Treatment: Exogenous PSK at 100 nM and 1 µM for 5 h
Key_finding: PSK produces ABA-axis +10.7 to +13.3%; P-axis negative; clusters with ABA/stress class not amino acids

Treatment: Exogenous PSK at 10 nM for 5 h
Key_finding: ABA-axis +14.5 to +16.8% in both tissues; Fe-axis strongly negative

Treatment: 185-member SynCom at high Pi (0.5 mM) or low Pi (0.05 mM)
Control: No-bacteria control at matched Pi concentration
Role_in_study: Pi-gating of ISR mode (Supp. Note S1)
Key_finding: Same SynCom: near-zero ISR at high Pi (JA -0.9%, SA +1.7%); strong ISR at low Pi (JA +9.3%, SA +14.5%)

Treatment: Synthetic bacterial community (SynCom) at low Pi (0.05 µM)
Key_finding: SynCom at low Pi: P +16.4%, JA +4.1%, SA +5.4%; confirms Pi-conditionality of PGPR P-axis response

Treatment: SynCom community with vs without Bacillus members
Role_in_study: Community composition effects on Bacillus fingerprint (Supp. Note S1)
Key_finding: Community lacking Bacillus: SA -30.2%, JA -13.6%; isolated Bacillus: SA +12.6% — opposite sign
Living SynCom33: SA +2.6% (ISR); heat-killed SynCom33: SA +0.005% (no ISR); ISR requires metabolically active bacteria

Treatment: Serendipita indica colonization at 3 and 10 days after inoculation
Role_in_study: Beneficial endophytic fungus fingerprinting (Supp. Note S2)
Key_finding: SA +4.9 to +8.0%, Fe +5.1 to +7.7% increasing with colonization; SA-ISR fingerprint, clusters with PGPR-SA subgroup

Treatment: Rhizophagus irregularis (AM fungi) at 0 mM or 150 mM NaCl
Control: Non-inoculated control at matched salt concentration
Role_in_study: Rice AMF validation — Pi-delivery vs Pi-sensing (Section 3.8)
Key_finding: AMF P-axis: -8.38% (suppressed, Pi delivery); PGPR P-axis: +11.8 to +13.7% (Pi-sensing enhanced); sign difference distinguishes mechanisms
